report_name: Sandhills Medical Foundation Discloses Ransomware Breach Affecting 169,000 Patients

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Ransomware Group
threat_actors.description: An unnamed threat actor who conducted a ransomware attack against Sandhills Medical Foundation in May 2025, accessing a server and exfiltrating and encrypting sensitive patient data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The attackers accessed and stole sensitive patient data from a server at Sandhills Medical Foundation.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors transferred stolen patient data out of the network before encrypting the files, as is typical in such attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The core ransomware activity where attackers encrypted files on a server to disrupt operations and demand a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The use of 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows' was noted as a cyber observable, indicating the attackers attempted to delete volume shadow copies to prevent system recovery.

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: A high volume of failed logins (Windows Event ID 4625) was suggested as a hunting hint, indicating a potential brute-force attempt to gain access to a server.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: The use of PowerShell with encoded commands was suggested as a hunting hint for detecting lateral movement and reconnaissance activities.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery, as noted in the hunting hints.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Suggested as a tool used for lateral movement and reconnaissance, often with encoded commands.

identities.name: Sandhills Medical Foundation, Inc.
identities.description: A healthcare provider that was the victim of a ransomware attack in May 2025, resulting in a data breach affecting 169,017 patients.

identities.name: Sandhills Medical Foundation Patients
identities.description: A group of 169,017 individuals whose PII and PHI, including Social Security numbers and medical records, were compromised in the ransomware attack on Sandhills Medical Foundation.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 5e8b1c4a-7d3f-4e9a-8b1c-4d7e8f9a0b1c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://classactionu.org/current-data-breaches/sandhills-medical-foundation-2/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a technique commonly used by ransomware to prevent system recovery.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of PowerShell with encoded commands, often used by attackers for obfuscation during lateral movement and reconnaissance.
references:
  - https://classactionu.org/current-data-breaches/sandhills-medical-foundation-2/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
      - ' -e '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell execution with encoded commands, a common technique for obfuscating malicious activity.

indicators.name: Anomalous Outbound Data Transfer
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Outbound Data Transfer to Cloud Storage
id: f9a8b7c6-d5e4-4f3a-2b1c-0d9e8f7a6b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects potentially large outbound data transfers to common cloud storage services, which could indicate data exfiltration prior to a ransomware attack.
references:
  - https://classactionu.org/current-data-breaches/sandhills-medical-foundation-2/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1048
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|contains:
      - 'mega.nz'
      - 'dropbox.com'
      - 'drive.google.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects large, unexpected data uploads to cloud storage services, which can be a sign of data exfiltration before ransomware deployment.

indicators.name: Multiple Failed Logon Attempts
indicators.pattern:
title: Multiple Failed Logon Attempts
id: 12345678-abcd-4ef0-9876-54321fedcba0
status: experimental
description: Detects a high volume of failed logon attempts (Event ID 4625), which could indicate a brute-force attack.
references:
  - https://classactionu.org/current-data-breaches/sandhills-medical-foundation-2/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1110
  - attack.credential_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4625
  condition: selection | count() > 20
indicators.description: Detects a high volume of failed logon attempts (Event ID 4625), which could be indicative of a brute-force attempt.
